bless your chart
  • about

094: NCAA Baseball Regionals

gtExtras
gt
Published

May 30, 2024

Load data

Code 
# notes from downloading the fthe data online
# https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/18iojV5_t-i9to4TUWxgBRUp8_fyPC0erROHm0icLAdE/edit#gid=0
# seed_data <- readr::read_csv("cws_seeds.csv") |> 
#  dplyr::rename(team_name = team)

# read in dsr data 
# https://d1baseball.com/diamond-sports-ranking/
# dsr <- readr::read_csv("dsr.csv") |> 
#  dplyr::rename(team_name = Team) 


# join the data 
# team_dsrs <- dplyr::inner_join(dsr, seed_data, by = "team_name") 

# RPI function
# rpi_ranks <- function(url) {

# rpi_page <- rvest::read_html(url)

# rpi_rk <- rpi_page |> 
#  rvest::html_nodes("table") |> 
#  rvest::html_table(fill = TRUE)

# rpi_table <- as.data.frame(rpi_rk) 

# rpi_table <- rpi_table |>
#  dplyr::mutate(record = stringr::str_split(Record, "-", simplify = T),
#                wins = record[,1],
#                losses = record[,2]
#  )
# }

# rpi <- rpi_ranks(url = "https://www.ncaa.com/rankings/baseball/d1/rpi")

# make rpi table
# ncaa_rpi <- rpi |>
#  dplyr::select(School, Rank) |>
#  dplyr::rename(team_name = School, rpi = Rank) |> 
#  dplyr::mutate(team_name = dplyr::case_match(team_name,
#                                "Oklahoma St." ~ "Oklahoma State",
#                                "Fresno St." ~ "Fresno State",
#                                "Florida St." ~ "Florida State",
#                                "Indiana St." ~ "Indiana State",
#                                "Army West Point" ~ "Army",
#                                "UNCW" ~ "UNC Wilmington",
#                                "Mississippi St." ~ "Mississippi State",
#                                "Oregon St." ~ "Oregon State",
#                                "DBU" ~ "Dallas Baptist",
#                                "UConn" ~ "Connecticut",
#                                "St. John's (NY)" ~ "St. John's",
#                                "LIU" ~ "Long Island",
#                                "Kansas St." ~ "Kansas State",
#                                "Louisiana" ~ "UL Lafayette",
#                                "Southern Miss." ~ "Southern Miss",
#                                "Southeast Mo. St." ~ "Southeast Missouri",
#                                "Western Mich." ~ "Western Michigan",
#                                "Northern Ky." ~ "Northern Kentucky",
#                                team_name ~ team_name
#                                 ))

# join the rest of the data
# team_dsrs_rpi <- team_dsrs |> 
#  dplyr::inner_join(ncaa_rpi, by = "team_name") 

# now add the wab data
# https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1OpvUmgjy854Mc_E96UA6B0NzwtxSP4D5w42wFn91rIY/edit#gid=28410142
# wab <- readr::read_csv("wab_2024.csv") |> 
# dplyr::select(team_name = School, wab_rank = Rank, WAB)

# final addition
# team_metrics <- team_dsrs_rpi |> 
#  dplyr::inner_join(wab, by = "team_name")

# logos 
# logos <- readr::read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kazink36/cfbplotR/main/data-raw/logo_ref_2.csv") |> 
#  dplyr::mutate(
#    school = dplyr::case_match(school,
#                               "Southeast Missouri State" ~ "Southeast Missouri",
#                               "Louisiana" ~ "UL Lafayette",
#                               "Pennsylvania" ~ "Penn",
#                               school ~ school)
#  ) |> 
#  dplyr::add_row(
#    school = "Dallas Baptist", 
#    logo = "https://a.espncdn.com/i/teamlogos/ncaa/500/2164.png") |> 
#  dplyr::add_row(
#    school = "Long Island", 
#    logo = "https://a.espncdn.com/i/teamlogos/ncaa/500/112358.png"
#  ) |> 
#  dplyr::rename(team_name = school)

# team_metrics |> 
#  dplyr::left_join(logos, by = "team_name") |> 
#  dplyr::mutate(bracket = dplyr::case_match(regional,
#                  "Chapel Hill" ~ "one",
#                  "Tucson" ~ "one", 
#                  "Charlottesville" ~ "one", 
#                  "Fayetteville" ~ "one",
#                  "Tallahasse" ~ "two", 
#                  "Norman" ~ "two",
#                  "Greenville" ~ "two",
#                  "Knoxville" ~ "two",
#                  "Lexington" ~ "three",
#                  "Corvallis" ~ "three",
#                  "Raleigh" ~ "three",
#                  "Athens" ~ "three",
#                  "Clemson" ~ "four", 
#                  "Stillwater" ~ "four",
#                  "Santa Barbara" ~ "four",
#                  "College Station" ~ "four")) |> 
#  dplyr::select(bracket, super, regional, seed, logo, team_name, `W-L-T`, WPCT, 
#                `DSR Rank`, rpi, `wab_rank`, `SOS Rank`) -> bracket_metrics

# read the odds from draftkings
# https://sportsbook.draftkings.com/leagues/baseball/ncaa-baseball?category=team-futures&subcategory=cws-regionals
bm <- readr::read_csv("bracket_metrics.csv") |> 
  dplyr::mutate(prob = dplyr::if_else(odds > 0,
                              100 / (odds + 100),
                              -odds / (-odds + 100))) |> 
  dplyr::select(-c(...1, odds, `SOS Rank`)) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(regional = paste0(regional, " Regional"))

GT Theme

Code 
gt_theme_chris <- function(gt_object, ...) {
  stopifnot("'gt_object' must be a 'gt_tbl', have you accidentally passed raw data?" = "gt_tbl" %in% class(gt_object))
  
  gt_object |> 
    gt::tab_options(
      heading.align = "left",
      table.border.top.style = "none",
      column_labels.border.bottom.style = "none",
      column_labels.border.bottom.color = "black",
      table_body.border.top.style = "none",
      table_body.border.bottom.color = "white",
      heading.border.bottom.style = "none",
      data_row.padding = gt::px(5),
      table.font.size = gt::px(12),
      column_labels.font.size = gt::px(12),
      column_labels.font.weight = "bolder",
      column_labels.border.bottom.width = gt::px(1),
      ...
    ) |> 
    gt::tab_style(
      style = gt::cell_text(
        color = "#333333",
        font = gt::google_font("Roboto"),
        transform = "uppercase"
      ),
      locations = list(
        gt::cells_column_labels(),
        gt::cells_stubhead()
      )
    ) |> 
    gt::tab_style(
      style = gt::cell_text(
        font = gt::google_font("Roboto"),
        weight = 800
      ),
      locations = gt::cells_title(groups = "title")
    ) |> 
    gt::tab_style(
      style = gt::cell_text(
        font = gt::google_font("Roboto"),
        weight = 400
      ),
      locations = gt::cells_body()
    ) |> 
    gt::tab_style(
      locations = gt::cells_title(groups = "subtitle"),
      style = gt::cell_text(
        size = "small"
      )
    ) |> 
    gt::tab_style(
      locations = gt::cells_title(groups = "title"),
      style = gt::cell_text(
        size = "large"
      )
    ) |> 
    gt::tab_style(
      locations = gt::cells_source_notes(),
      style = gt::cell_text(
        size = "x-small"
      )
    ) |> 
    gt::tab_style(
      style = gt::cell_borders(sides = "top", color = 'black', weight = gt::px(1.5), style = 'solid'),
      locations = gt::cells_body(
        rows = gt::everything()
      )
    )  |> 
    gt::tab_style(
      locations = gt::cells_row_groups(groups = gt::everything()),
      style = list(
        gt::cell_text(
          size = "small",
          weight = "bold"
        ), 
        gt::cell_fill(color = "#FFFEE0")
      )) |> 
    gt::tab_style(
      style = list(
        gt::cell_fill(color = "#FFFEE0"),
        gt::cell_text(color = "#333333", size = gt::px(12), weight = "bold"),
        gt::cell_borders(sides = "right", color = "black", weight = gt::px(2)),
        gt::cell_borders(sides = "bottom", color = "black", weight = gt::px(2))
      ),
      locations = list(
        gt::cells_column_spanners(
          spanners = gt::everything()
        )
      )
    )
}

Function to make the GT tables

Code 
ncaab_tbl <- function(bracket) {
  
  # Set the title of the plot
  table_title <- "College World Series: Regionals by the Numbers"
  
  bm |>
    dplyr::filter(bracket == {
      {
        bracket
      }
    }) |>
    dplyr::arrange(seed, super) |> 
    dplyr::relocate(prob, .before = `DSR Rank`) |> 
    gt::gt(groupname_col = "regional") |> 
    gtExtras::gt_img_rows(columns = logo, 
                          img_source = "web", height = 30) |> 
    gt::cols_hide(columns = c(bracket, super)) |> 
    gt::cols_label(
      seed = "",
      logo = "",
      team_name = "",
      `W-L-T` = "Record",
      WPCT = "Win %",
      prob = "Odds",
      `DSR Rank` = "DSR",
      rpi = "RPI",
      wab_rank = "WAB",
    ) |>
    gt_theme_chris() |> 
    gt::tab_spanner(label = "Rankings", 
                    columns = c(`DSR Rank`,
                                wab_rank, rpi)) |> 
    gt::cols_align(align = "left", columns = c(logo, team_name)) |>
    gtExtras::gt_hulk_col_numeric(columns = c(`DSR Rank`,
                                              wab_rank, rpi),
                                  reverse = TRUE) |>
    gtExtras::gt_hulk_col_numeric(columns = c(prob)) |>
    gt::fmt_percent(columns = c(prob), decimals = 1) |> 
    gtExtras::gt_add_divider(columns = c(WPCT, prob), 
                             sides = "right", color = "black") |> 
    gt::tab_header(title = table_title,
                   subtitle = gt::html("Regionals grouped by national seeds. Data inclues overall record, win percentage, odds to win regional, and numerical rankings across various D-I metrics.")) |>
    gt::tab_source_note(source_note = gt::html("<hr><br>
                                       Odds to win regional from DraftKings<br>
                                       DSR is the Diamond Sports Ranking computed by 643charts.com<br>
                                       RPI is from the stats.ncaa.org<br>
                                       WAB is wins-above-bubble computed by Seth Burn<br>
                                       <hr>
                                       <b>Table by Chris (@dadgumboxscores) + Bless your chart | | May 30, 2024</b>")) |> 
    gt::tab_style(
      style = gt::cell_borders(sides = "top", color = 'black', weight = gt::px(1.5), style = 'solid'),
      locations = gt::cells_body(
        rows = gt::everything()
      )
    ) -> ncaabt_table
  
    ncaabt_table
} 

ncaab_tbl(bracket = "one") -> tbl_one

ncaab_tbl(bracket = "two") -> tbl_two

ncaab_tbl(bracket = "three") -> tbl_three

ncaab_tbl(bracket = "four") -> tbl_four

gtExtras::gtsave_extra(tbl_one,
                       filename = "nccat_tbl_one.png",
                       vheight = 1200,
                       vwidth = 750)

gtExtras::gtsave_extra(tbl_two,
                       filename = "nccat_tbl_twos.png",
                       vheight = 1200,
                       vwidth = 750)

gtExtras::gtsave_extra(tbl_three,
                       filename = "nccat_tbl_three.png",
                       vheight = 1200,
                       vwidth = 750)

gtExtras::gtsave_extra(tbl_four,
                       filename = "nccat_tbl_four.png",
                       vheight = 1200,
                       vwidth = 750)

# example!
tbl_one
College World Series: Regionals by the Numbers
Regionals grouped by national seeds. Data inclues overall record, win percentage, odds to win regional, and numerical rankings across various D-I metrics.
Record Win % Odds Rankings
DSR WAB RPI
Tucson Regional
1 Arizona 36-21-0 0.632 36.4% 36 24 31
2 Dallas Baptist 44-13-0 0.772 36.4% 24 25 17
3 West Virginia 33-22-0 0.600 23.3% 41 35 37
4 Grand Canyon 34-23-0 0.596 13.3% 93 82 96
Chapel Hill Regional
1 North Carolina 42-13-0 0.764 52.4% 2 4 4
2 LSU 40-21-0 0.656 47.6% 10 19 23
3 Wofford 41-18-0 0.695 10.0% 50 69 46
4 Long Island 33-23-0 0.589 1.0% 187 188 195
Fayetteville Regional
1 Arkansas 43-14-0 0.754 65.5% 6 5 5
2 Louisiana Tech 44-17-0 0.721 18.2% 33 41 29
3 Kansas State 32-24-0 0.571 22.2% 66 40 45
4 Southeast Missouri 34-25-0 0.576 3.8% 106 159 132
Charlottesville Regional
1 Virginia 41-15-0 0.732 54.5% 9 11 12
2 Mississippi State 38-21-0 0.644 43.5% 17 21 25
3 St. John's 37-16-1 0.694 7.1% 73 64 51
4 Penn 24-23-0 0.511 3.8% 161 167 180

Odds to win regional from DraftKings
DSR is the Diamond Sports Ranking computed by 643charts.com
RPI is from the stats.ncaa.org
WAB is wins-above-bubble computed by Seth Burn
Table by Chris (@dadgumboxscores) + Bless your chart | | May 30, 2024
